claims.claim: For the non-cancellative toy monoid M = <a,t | t+a=t>, the Grothendieck group completion is K(M) isomorphic to Z, generated by [t], with [a]=0 (Example, 'a non-cancellative toy').
claims.location: Example ex:noncanc (Sec. Group completion); also Sec. Formal verification (Main.hs)
claims.evidence: Verified directly. gamma(a)=gamma(0) since a+t=0+t (witness e=t), so [a]=0. K(M) is generated by [t] because a,t generate M and [a]=0. [t] has infinite order: n[t]=0 would require nt+e=e for some e in M; checking both normal-form families (e=ma gives nt+ma=nt != ma; e=kt gives (n+k)t != kt for n>=1) shows no solution, exactly the paper's stated fact 'nt+e=e has no solution for n>=1'. Hence K(M)=Z<[t]>. This is the correct fix of the previously flagged toy-monoid completion bug (the completion is Z generated by [t], not the trivial/zero group).

claims.claim: The Grothendieck group completion K(M)=(MxM)/~ of a commutative monoid exists, is an abelian group, gamma_M is a monoid hom, and satisfies the universal property (Thm, universal property); gamma_M(a)=gamma_M(b) iff a+e=b+e for some e, the largest injective quotient is the cancellative M^st, and K(M)=K(M^st) (Prop, stabilization element).
claims.location: Definition def:groth, Theorem thm:groth, Proposition prop:stable (Sec. Group completion)
claims.evidence: Full proof checked. Transitivity uses the explicit witness e''=d+e+e'; substituting the two hypotheses a+d+e=c+b+e and c+h+e'=g+d+e' yields a+h+e''=g+b+e'' as claimed. Well-definedness of addition, inverse -[(a,b)]=[(b,a)] via (a+b,a+b)~(0,0), and the induced bar-f([(a,b)])=f(a)-f(b) (well defined because A is a group, so the '+e' term cancels) are all correct. prop:stable's cancellativity of M^st and the kernel-pair identification giving K(M^st) iso K(M) are correct. This is the standard Grothendieck construction with the '+e' relation needed for non-cancellative M; the physical reading of e as the ancilla-stabilization element is sound.

claims.claim: Stacking boxtimes makes (Phases_{d,G}, boxtimes, [triv]) a commutative monoid whose invertible elements are exactly the SRE phases (Prop, commutative monoid; Prop, invertible elements are SRE).
claims.location: Proposition prop:monoid, Proposition prop:sre (Sec. Stacking as a symmetric monoidal structure)
claims.evidence: The pi_0 monoid laws are verified elementarily and correctly: respect for W (finite-depth tensor finite-depth is finite-depth; QAC of a tensor-sum path is the tensor of continuations, cited to Hastings-Wen and BMNS), associativity/commutativity via the depth-zero on-site associator/swap (with correct Koszul-sign remark for fermions), and [triv] as two-sided unit. prop:sre is the definitional statement that invertibles form a full symmetric-monoidal subgroupoid. The paper correctly scopes the claim: only the pi_0 monoid structure is proven; the full infinity-symmetric-monoidal structure is deferred to Conjecture I-1 and to cited parametrized-spin-system work (beaudry-etal). Honest and correct.

claims.claim: If the invertible sector inv_{d,G} is a Picard infinity-groupoid (grouplike E-infinity space) then there is a unique connective spectrum IPcond_{d,G} with Omega^infinity IPcond = inv_{d,G} and the stated homotopy groups; Kubota's Omega-spectrum is a rigorous carrier for its homotopy groups (Thm, recognition; Thm, Kubota).
claims.location: Theorem thm:recognition, Definition def:ipcond, Theorem thm:kubota (Sec. The invertible condensed phase spectrum)
claims.evidence: thm:recognition is a correct invocation of the recognition principle (the infinity-category of connective spectra is equivalent to grouplike E-infinity spaces via Omega^infinity), and it is explicitly marked conditional on the grouplike hypothesis, which itself rests on Conjecture I-1 of Part I. pi_0 IPcond = Phases^x (already group-completed since grouplike) and pi_n matching for n>=1 are correct. The recognition-principle citations are used appropriately: beaudry-etal (Homotopical Foundations of Parametrized Quantum Spin Systems) for parametrized foundations and kubota-omega-spectrum (Kubota, 'Stable homotopy theory of invertible gapped quantum spin systems I: Kitaev's Omega-spectrum', 2025) as the explicit operator-algebraic carrier realizing Kitaev's proposal. Both bib entries exist and match the paper's description. The conditional framing is honest.

claims.claim: The trivial system triv has no excited states and is assigned gap +infinity by convention, so stacking with triv never lowers a gap; stacking two uniquely-gapped systems gives gap = min(Delta_1, Delta_2) (Def, stacking; Lemma, stacking preserves the gapped substack).
claims.location: Definition def:stacking, Lemma lem:gapstack (Sec. The stabilized phase stack)
claims.evidence: The +infinity assignment is a legitimate and standard convention: triv has a one-dimensional total Hilbert space with no excited state, so the spectral gap above the ground state is vacuously +infinity, making min(Delta, +infinity)=Delta. The finite-volume computation is correct: H1 tensor 1 + 1 tensor H2 has spectrum {lambda+mu}, unique product ground state, first excited energy min(E1_1+E0_2, E0_1+E1_2), so gap = min(Delta_1,Delta_2). The thermodynamic-limit statement is correctly deferred to Part III's infinite-volume GNS construction, appropriate in a series. No correctness issue.

claims.claim: The tenfold-way invariant groups G(s,d)=d_bullet((s-d) mod p) are Bott periodic (8-fold real, 2-fold complex) and antidiagonally invariant under (s,d)->(s+1,d+1); Table 1 lists them (Prop, Bott periodicity; Def, tenfold way; Table 1).
claims.location: Definition def:tenfold, Proposition prop:bott, Table tab:tenfold (Sec. Evidence)
claims.evidence: Verified exhaustively. The real Bott sequence d_R=(Z, Z/2, Z/2, 0, 2Z, 0, 0, 0) equals KO_*(pt) (the 2Z at index 4 being the standard even-labelling convention, stated in the paper); d_C=(Z,0)=KU_*(pt). All 80 table entries were recomputed from G(s,d)=d_bullet((s-d) mod p) with the Bott-clock ordering AI=0,BDI=1,D=2,DIII=3,AII=4,CII=5,C=6,CI=7 (real) and A=0,AIII=1 (complex); every row matches Table 1. All landmark entries verified against known physics: D/d=1 Kitaev chain Z/2, D/d=2 p+ip Z, AII/d=2,3 QSH+3D-TI Z/2, A/d=2 IQHE Z, AIII/d=1 SSH Z. The antidiagonal invariance is tautological from the s-d dependence and the paper correctly attributes the physical identification (dimensional reduction; d->d+1 as the Bott generator) to cited K-theory results rather than reproving it. Machine-checked per Sec. Formal verification.

claims.claim: The comparison map c_{d,G}: IPcond_{d,G} -> I_FH exists as a map of spectra and is conjectured (Conjecture IV-2) to be an equivalence, under SRE hypotheses, at levels (L0)/(L1)/(L2) (Def, comparison map; Conjecture IV-2).
claims.location: Definition def:comp, Conjecture conj:iv2 (Sec. The comparison conjecture)
claims.evidence: The central point is handled correctly: the EQUIVALENCE (IV-2) is explicitly and repeatedly marked open at all three levels and is never claimed proven, and the paper is careful that (L0) is a theorem only in two regimes while (L1)/(L2) have targets but no identification. The only overreach is framing: def:comp is stated as a Definition asserting 'the map of spectra c_{d,G}', but the map's existence rests on (i) IPcond being a spectrum at all, which thm:recognition marks conditional on Conjecture I-1, and (ii) an 'extension to a map of spectra by naturality in d and the symmetry data' that is asserted, not constructed (Freed's SRE-to-TQFT assignment is invoked but not built into a rigorous spectrum-level map). The prose does hedge ('we isolate it as a hypothesis'), so this is a presentation nuance rather than a false theorem, but a reader could take the Definition as a rigorous construction.
claims.suggested_fix: Reword def:comp to flag that the existence of c_{d,G} as a map of spectra is itself conditional on Conjecture I-1 (for IPcond to be a spectrum) and on the naturality of Freed's assignment, i.e. present the map's construction as part of the hypothesis of IV-2 rather than as an unconditional Definition (e.g. 'Assuming IPcond is a spectrum, there is a comparison map ...').

claims.claim: In d=1 the operator-algebraic split-property index is a complete invariant valued in H^2(G,T), so the (L0) comparison is a bijection (theorem); the analogous 2D completeness is Ogata's H^3(G,T)-index (Thm, d=1 comparison and its discussion).
claims.location: Theorem thm:d1 and following discussion (Sec. Evidence)
claims.evidence: The d=1 statement is correct and correctly scoped: Ogata's completeness of the split-property index valued in H^2(G,T) matches the group-cohomology classification of (1+1)D bosonic SPTs, giving a genuine (L0) bijection; the paper honestly notes this touches neither (L1) nor (L2). The parenthetical extension 'In d=2 the analogous completeness is Ogata's H^3(G,T)-index ... it gives the (L0) comparison for 2D on-site finite symmetry as well' overstates the state of the art: Ogata's 2D construction produces an H^3(G,T)-valued index, but its COMPLETENESS (that it is a full invariant classifying all 2D SPT phases) is not established at the level the 1D split-property result is. Calling it 'completeness' asserts more than the cited literature supports.
claims.suggested_fix: Soften the 2D remark to state that Ogata constructs an H^3(G,T)-valued index in d=2 (giving a well-defined comparison homomorphism at pi_0) without asserting it is a complete invariant, or add a caveat that 2D completeness is not known.

claims.claim: Reflection-positive invertible d-dimensional field theories of symmetry type H_d are classified by [MTH, Sigma^{d+1} I_Z], homotopy classes of spectrum maps into a shift of the Anderson dual of the sphere; assembling gives the spectrum I_FH (Def, Freed-Hopkins target).
claims.evidence: Correct statement of the Freed-Hopkins classification: the deformation-class group is [MTH, Sigma^{d+1} I_Z] with I_Z the Anderson dual of the sphere and MTH the Madsen-Tillmann spectrum of the tangential structure; the degree shift d+1 is right. The characterization of the torsion part as 'beyond group cohomology' and the free part as integer invariants (Hall conductances, chiral central charges) is accurate. Bib entry freed-hopkins ('Reflection positivity and invertible topological phases', Geometry & Topology) matches.

claims.claim: For a 2D SRE lattice state the Hall conductance is locally computable, an integer multiple of e^2/h, constant on gapped phases, hence a homomorphism Phases^x_{2,G} -> Z, with a higher-Berry generalization for families (Thm, Hall conductance as a phase invariant).
claims.location: Theorem thm:hall (Sec. Lattice invariants, SSH, transitions)
claims.evidence: This is a cited result (Kapustin-Sopenko), correctly reported: for invertible/SRE states the locally-computable Hall conductance is integer-quantized (fractional values require topological order, excluded here) and constant on phases, giving the stated homomorphism to Z = pi_0 I_FH in class A. The identification with the free part of the Freed-Hopkins target and the placement 'on the honest side of the Kapustin-Fidkowski wall' (chiral states not realized by commuting-projector models) are consistent with Part V's scope. The map implicitly requires a U(1) charge symmetry, which is the standard setting and a negligible notational point.

claims.claim: The SSH chain with q(k)=t_1+t_2 e^{ik} is gapped iff |t_1| != |t_2|, is class AIII in d=1 with Z invariant the winding number (nu=1 for |t_1|<|t_2|, nu=0 for |t_1|>|t_2|), and its low-energy theory is a massive Dirac fermion whose mass sign is the winding; c_{1,AIII} matches lattice and EFT at (L0) (Example, SSH).
claims.location: Example ex:ssh (Sec. Lattice invariants, SSH, transitions)
claims.evidence: All checks pass. H(k)=Re(q) sigma_x + Im(q) sigma_y with q=t_1+t_2 e^{ik}, so E_pm=+/-|q(k)| and the gap closes iff min_k |q|=||t_1|-|t_2||=0, i.e. |t_1|=|t_2|; representative closing at t_1=t_2, k=pi (q(pi)=t_1-t_2). The winding of q:S^1->C^x around 0 is 1 when the radius |t_2| exceeds the offset |t_1| and 0 otherwise, matching the stated nu. SSH has chiral/sublattice symmetry => class AIII, table entry Z, consistent with TC6. The Dirac-mass-sign = winding statement is the standard massive 1+1D Dirac picture. Correct.

claims.claim: The five substantive extrapolations are stated as numbered Conjectures IV-1 (condensed/solid refinement), IV-2 (lattice-EFT comparison), IV-3 (relative charge = spectral boundary map), IV-4 (solidification commutes with group completion), IV-5 (renormalization functor), none claimed as theorems.
claims.location: Conjectures conj:iv1, conj:iv2, conj:iv3, conj:iv4, conj:iv5 (throughout)
claims.evidence: All five are in Conjecture environments with stable IV-n identifiers and are never upgraded to theorems; the Discussion section reiterates their status and the SRE/undecidability limitations (IV-5 restricted to the SRE stratum because of Cubitt-Perez-Garcia-Wolf undecidability; noninvertible order explicitly out of scope). One minor stylistic note: the IV-4 plausibility remark ('K is a left adjoint and solidification is a left adjoint, and left adjoints compose') conflates composition of adjoints with the interchange/commutation actually needed, but the paper immediately states 'the interchange is not automatic' and keeps IV-4 a conjecture, so no correctness problem. The honest theorem/conjecture separation is the paper's principal strength.
